//: Solution-style root config. `files` stays empty on purpose: this project
compiles nothing itself, it only names the four real projects so that a
single `tsc -b` at the root typechecks the whole monorepo.
Until this list was populated, `references` was empty too, which made
`tsc -b` fail with TS18002 ('the files list in this config file is empty')
and exit non-zero. `pnpm typecheck` had therefore never typechecked
anything, and apps/web in particular was covered by no command at all.
The referenced projects are deliberately NOT `composite`. Every one of them
sets noEmit, so there are no declaration outputs to wire together and no
build ordering to respect; TypeScript resolves cross-package imports
straight through the workspace source. Adding composite would buy
incremental rebuild artifacts we do not need and would force declaration
emit on packages that are consumed as source.
